channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Teens slashed adults who told them to be quiet during movie, police say
item.description: According to police, the boys, who were between the ages of 14 and 16, got upset after they were told to be quiet during a movie.
item.dc:creator: Melanie DaSilva, NewsNation

item.title: Judge finds probable cause to hold Trump administration in contempt for violating deportation order
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday said he has found probable cause to hold the Trump administration in criminal contempt of court for violating his orders to turn around planes carrying deportees to El Salvador. U.S. District Judge James E.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Boasberg moves to hold Trump administration in contempt over deportation flights
item.description: A federal judge Wednesday found probable cause to hold the Trump administration in criminal contempt for willfully disobeying his order to immediately halt deportations under the rarely used Alien Enemies Act and turn around any airborne planes. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, ABC 4

item.title: AmeriCorps members who respond to disasters and help nonprofits let go in DOGE cuts
item.description: Young volunteers who respond to natural disasters and help with community projects across the U.S. have been discharged.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Fingerhut, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: MrBeast fans seek refunds after Las Vegas event
item.description: A crowd of frustrated "MrBeast" fans gathered Tuesday in Las Vegas to demand refunds for a three-day experience.
item.dc:creator: Jezzamine Wolk, WGN 9

item.title: DOJ announces civil suit against Maine over transgender athletes
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi on Tuesday announced a civil lawsuit against Maine’s Department of Education for violating Title IX, the federal law against sex discrimination that the Trump administration has said prohibits transgender students from participating in girls’ and women’s sports in schools.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, WGN 9

item.title: Seth Rogen calls out Silicon Valley's Trump support in cut awards show remarks: Report
item.description: “And it’s amazing that others [who have been] in this room underwrote electing a man who, in the last week, single-handedly destroyed all of American science,” Rogen, 43, said.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Visa cancellations sow panic for international students, with hundreds fearing deportation
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — At first, the bar association for immigration attorneys began receiving inquiries from a couple students a day. These were foreigners studying in the U.S., and they'd discovered in early April their legal status had been terminated with little notice.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, MAKIYA SEMINERA and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: White House removes wire spot from press pool
item.description: The White House is making changes to which outlets are included in the press pool covering President Trump, and doing away with the spot normally reserved for wire services covering his daily activities.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Starbucks introducing new dress code for baristas, a move protested by some
item.description: Baristas at your local Starbucks will soon be sporting a new, more consistent look after the company announced a new dress code this week.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KRON4

item.title: Man in tactical gear attacks officers with pepper spray at Boston-area courthouse, video shows
item.description: Police in a Boston suburb have arrested a man who they say entered a courthouse while wearing a gas mask and tactical gear and attacked several people with pepper spray.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wink Martindale, the genial game show host and an early TV interviewer of Elvis Presley, dies at 91
item.description: Wink Martindale, the genial host of such hit game shows as “Gambit” and “Tic-Tac-Dough” who also did one of the first recorded television interviews with a young Elvis Presley, has died. He was 91.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bryan Kohberger defense aims to create 'illusion' with renewed bid to toss evidence: fmr. FBI agent
item.description: A former FBI special agent says that Bryan Kohberger's defense team is trying to create an "illusion" for future jurors with a renewed effort to get some evidence tossed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspect in shooting at Dallas high school is in custody, officials say
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — A suspect in a shooting at a Dallas high school that wounded four students and drew a heavy police response to the campus has been taken into custody, school district officials announced Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fort Benning takes back its old name, but to honor a different soldier
item.description: The Army is restoring the name Fort Benning to its storied training post in Georgia, only this time to honor an 18-year-old corporal who fought in World War I rather than a Confederate general. A ceremony to make the name change official was scheduled Wednesday at the base just outside Columbus.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE KRAMON and RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about Hollywood icon Gene Hackman's and his wife's deaths
item.description: Authorities in northern New Mexico released police body camera video and other public records Tuesday in the investigation into the deaths of Gene Hackman and his wife, Betsy Arakawa. The two were found dead Feb. 26 in their Santa Fe home.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ten Commandments in classrooms 'grave concern' to Baptist pastor
item.description: A bill requiring the display of the Ten Commandments in public buildings and classrooms was signed into law in Arkansas on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Member of Zizian group says she did not kill her parents
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — The daughter of a Pennsylvania couple whose deaths are among six connected to a cultlike group says she has been falsely accused of killing her parents.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NTSB finds 'failure' in training before deadly Newark cargo ship fire
item.description: The Newark fire department's lack of training and familiarity with marine firefighting during a deadly cargo ship fire at the East Coast's largest port in July 2023 amounted to a “failure of leadership,” the head of the National Transportation Safety Board said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man arrested outside UnitedHealthcare is now charged with firearms counts and threatening violence
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A man who was arrested outside the headquarters of UnitedHealthcare had a history of mental health issues and threatened to shoot up corporate facilities, according to charges filed Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Actor William Levy arrested after drunken 'disturbance' at Florida restaurant: report
item.description: Cuban-American actor William Levy was arrested in South Florida Monday after causing a "disturbance" inside a restaurant, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump not expected to attend White House Correspondents' dinner
item.description: President Donald Trump is not expected to attend next weekend's White House Correspondents' dinner, The Hill confirmed on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KTLA 5

item.title: Man who says he was held captive in Connecticut home for 20 years makes his first public comments
item.description: A man who told police that he was held captive in a Connecticut home by his father and stepmother and given limited food for 20 years before being rescued is thanking medical staff for holding what he called his first-ever birthday party as he turned 32.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man allegedly held captive by stepmother in Connecticut releases first public statement
item.description: In his first public statement, the man allegedly held captive in his home for over 20 years by his stepmother says he's been getting stronger as he begins the process of reclaiming his life.
item.dc:creator: Bailey Wright, WGN 9

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel: Liberals 'viciously attacking comedians' are a 'big part of why Trump' won
item.description: "I think a lot of the outrage is completely manufactured, and it’s like, a lot of these people who are angry aren’t really angry," Kimmel said.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Georgia man arrested over alleged threats to kill Tulsi Gabbard
item.description: “Threatening to harm public officials is a criminal act that cannot be excused as political discourse,” acting U.S. Attorney Richard Moultrie, Jr. said.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Man allegedly held captive by stepmother releases first public statement
item.description: "I am speaking out today to begin the process of reclaiming my life and to have my say in how my story is told," he said.
item.dc:creator: Bailey Wright, NewsNation

item.title: Florida man confronts neighbor accused of shooting protected bird, video shows
item.description: A heated confrontation between a Florida homeowner and his neighbor over the shooting of a protected bird has prompted outrage in the community.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Why every MLB player will wear the same number Tuesday
item.description: Every MLB player, regardless of team or position, will be wearing the exact same jersey number on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Gov. Cox declares 'Declutter Day' in Utah
item.description: The Utah Division of Consumer Protection and Gov. Spencer J. Cox declared April 18, 2025 as the official Declutter Day in Utah.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Baggerly, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration freezes $2.2 billion in federal funds for Harvard University
item.description: The Trump administration froze $2.2 billion worth of federal money for Harvard University after the school said it would not cave to the White House’s orders to limit activism or political speech on campus.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Shirley, KTLA 5

item.title: Southern Utah fire chief's trespass charge dismissed after donation, apology letter
item.description: Hurricane Fire Chief Joe Decker's charge was dismissed after he submitted a letter of apology and made a financial donation.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4

item.title: 5.2 magnitude earthquake shakes Southern California
item.description: The quake's epicenter was around two miles south of the community of Julian, about 60 miles east of San Diego.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, WGN 9

item.title: Trump says he's open to sending violent criminals who are US citizens to El Salvador prison
item.description: President Trump on Monday indicated he would be open to sending American citizens who are violent criminals to El Salvador to be held in a notorious prison there, telling reporters his administration is studying law on the matter.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, ABC 4

item.title: 2022 NCAA woman of the year, family members killed in upstate New York plane crash
item.description: A private plane that crashed in upstate New York over the weekend was carrying six members of a close-knit family of physicians and distinguished student-athletes on a trip to the Catskills for a birthday celebration and the Passover holiday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 3 cases of rare brain disease reported in Oregon
item.description: Three cases of a rare brain disease have been reported by public health officials in Hood River County.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Flanigan, KTLA 5

item.title: Texas man suing NFL player over December shooting at car lot
item.description: A Texas man who says Titans cornerback L'Jarius Sneed or his personal assistant shot at him while he was sitting inside a Mercedes-Benz on a dealer lot in December is suing for at least $1 million.
item.dc:creator: Teresa M. Walker, Fox 5
